response.justification: The candidate is a currently-titled Staff Engineer doing AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling work, which directly matches this AI-native environment, and he has genuine high-throughput distributed-systems experience from a micro-services platform handling millions of requests per second. However, Python — a must-have — appears nowhere on the resume, his distributed-systems depth is several years old and rooted in the niche DLang ecosystem, and the resume shows little concrete evidence of staff-level technical strategy, cross-functional stakeholder leadership, or operational/on-call ownership. Worth a conversation if the Python gap is bridgeable, but not a clear yes.
strengths: Current hands-on work on AI-agent infrastructure, MCP code generators, and agentic developer tooling at Naver
Real high-throughput distributed-systems experience (low-latency services handling millions of HTTP requests per second)
Strong, recent TypeScript/Node.js depth across product and platform work, plus solid engineering-craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development)
concerns: No Python anywhere in the skills list or work history, despite strong Python being a must-have
Limited evidence of staff-scope technical strategy, cross-functional stakeholder management, or incident-response/on-call ownership
Deepest high-throughput systems work is from 2018-2020 in DLang; recent roles skew toward full-stack product engineering
